schema.name: jobReqId
schema.description: Unique identifier for each job requisition.

schema.name: age
schema.description: Age of the job requisition.

schema.name: appTemplateId
schema.description: Identifier for the application template used for the job requisition.

schema.name: cust_Department
schema.description: Department associated with the job requisition.

schema.name: cust_Location
schema.description: Location associated with the job requisition.

schema.name: cust_Division
schema.description: Division associated with the job requisition.

schema.name: internalStatus
schema.description: Internal status of the job requisition.

schema.name: jobProfile_externalCode
schema.description: External code for the job profile associated with the job requisition.

schema.name: templateName
schema.description: Name of the template used for the job requisition.

schema.name: templateType
schema.description: Type of template used for the job requisition.

schema.name: createdDateTime
schema.description: Timestamp indicating when the job requisition was created.

schema.name: lastModifiedDateTime
schema.description: Timestamp indicating when the job requisition was last modified.

schema.name: scd_start_date
schema.description: Record validity start date.

schema.name: scd_end_date
schema.description: Record validity end date (NULL if current).

schema.name: is_current
schema.description: Flag for the latest record.

schema.name: record_hash
schema.description: Hash to detect changes.

description: This table offers a comprehensive view into the organization's job requisition process, providing users with critical insights into hiring demands and trends. Users can retrieve detailed information about each job requisition, including its unique identifier (jobReqId), its current age (duration since creation), and the appTemplateId used for the application. The table clearly outlines the organizational context of each requisition, specifying the cust_Department, cust_Location, and cust_Division where the role is based. The internalStatus column keeps users informed about the current stage of the requisition (e.g., 'Approved', 'Closed'). Further details include the jobProfile_externalCode, templateName, and templateType, which help categorize and understand the nature of the roles being recruited. Timestamps such as createdDateTime and lastModifiedDateTime allow for tracking the requisition's lifecycle. Importantly, this table also maintains a historical record of changes through scd_start_date, scd_end_date, and is_current flags, enabling users to analyze how job requisitions evolve over time. This data is invaluable for HR professionals, recruiters, and strategic planners to understand hiring patterns, assess departmental needs, and optimize recruitment strategies.

description: This table is a foundational resource for understanding the organizational structure and how employees are integrated within it. Users can access a detailed mapping of each employee (EmployeeCode) to their specific organizational units, providing a clear picture of who belongs where. The table breaks down the organizational hierarchy into SectorCode/SectorName, BusinessUnitCode/BusinessUnitName, DivisionCode/DivisionName, SubDivisionCode/SubDivisionName, DepartmentCode/DepartmentName, and SubDepartmentCode/SubDepartmentName. This granular detail allows users to analyze workforce distribution across various levels of the organization. Beyond structure, it provides crucial employee-specific information such as JobTitle, EmployeeGrade, Location, and Country, offering insights into roles and geographical distribution. The EffectiveStartDate indicates when an employee's assignment to a particular organizational unit became active, which is vital for historical analysis and tracking changes over time. Additional fields like org_id, org_name, Mapped_Business_Sector, and company_code further enrich the organizational context. This table is essential for HR analytics, organizational reporting, workforce planning, and understanding the composition and distribution of the employee base.

description: This table provides a focused and critical dataset for managing and analyzing employee maternity leave. Users can obtain specific information about employees who have taken maternity leave, including their employee_id, first_name, and last_name. The table details the organizational context of these employees, such as their department, sector_name, and Mapped_Business_Sector. The core of this table lies in its maternity leave-specific attributes: maternity_leave_type categorizes the nature of the leave, while maternity_start_date, maternity_end_date, and maternity_return_date provide precise timelines for each leave period. This information is invaluable for HR departments to track ongoing leaves, plan for employee returns, and manage workforce capacity during leave periods. Furthermore, the data supports comprehensive analysis of maternity leave patterns within the organization, helping to identify trends, assess the impact on various departments, and ensure equitable and compliant leave management. It also plays a crucial role in fulfilling regulatory reporting requirements and supporting employee well-being initiatives.

description: This table offers a rich and detailed dataset covering the entire journey of job applications within the organization. Users can gain deep insights into individual applications and broader hiring trends. For each application, the table provides a unique applicationId and links to the candidateId and jobReqId, establishing connections across the hiring ecosystem. Personal details of candidates, including firstName, lastName, gender, contactEmail, and cellPhone, are available. The appStatusName column is particularly useful, indicating the current stage of each application (e.g., 'Rejected - Email', 'Interview Scheduled'), allowing users to monitor progress and identify bottlenecks. Information about the source of the application helps in evaluating the effectiveness of different recruitment channels. Timestamps like candidateCreationDate and lastModifiedDateTime provide a chronological understanding of the application process. Crucially, the table also captures interview-related data, including interview_startDate, interview_averageRating, interview_overallRating, interview_comments, and the interviewer_userId. This comprehensive data enables users to analyze recruitment funnel efficiency, assess candidate quality, evaluate interviewer performance, and refine overall hiring strategies.

description: This table serves as the definitive master record for all employee-related information, offering a holistic view of the workforce. Users can access a wealth of data covering employee demographics, employment history, and current job attributes. Key identifiers include employee_id, full_name, first_name, last_name, and both work_email and personal_email. The table meticulously tracks employment events, detailing the employment_event_reason and event_date, alongside crucial dates like group_joining_date, joining_date, resignation_date, and last_working_date. This allows for comprehensive analysis of employee tenure and transitions. Organizational details are extensively covered, including department, sub_department, division, vertical, location, country, business_unit, and company_name, providing a clear picture of where employees are situated within the corporate structure. Job-specific information such as job_title and job_grade is also available. Furthermore, the table includes demographic data like birth_date and gender, and critical management information such as manager_name, manager_email, hr_business_partner_name, and hr_business_partner_email. The employee_status and employee_status_code provide current employment status. This table is indispensable for all HR functions, including payroll, benefits, talent management, compliance, and strategic workforce planning, offering a single source of truth for employee data.
